title: Cockroach Janta Party protest LIVE | Protest continues for second day; Abhijeet Dipke urges people to join
content: Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) protesters asserted that they would not leave until Dharmendra Pradhan resigns

title: Day 3 of BEST strike: Mumbai bus operations hit; 100 buses deployed for NEET students
content: The strike, called by the BEST Sanyukt Kamgar Kruti Samiti, has virtually paralysed the civic-run bus transport system since June 19, forcing passengers to depend on other transportation methods for their daily commute

title: Cyber police book controversial YouTuber Thoppi for allegedly posting obscene content
content: Thoppi allegedly posted nude images of his friends on social media after he had a fallout with the latter

title: Thousands perform yoga at Mysore palace as city celebrates International Yoga Day
content: The grand yoga session, held under this year’s theme, ‘Yoga for Healthy Ageing’, showcased Mysuru’s lasting legacy as a global hub of modern yoga and drew participation from people across all age groups

title: Yoga is more than a physical exercise, it’s a source of consciousness, vitality, and inner energy: PM
content: The Prime Minister observed that when we speak of yoga for healthy ageing, it signifies a collective commitment to ensuring that advancing age does not diminish human potential.

title: Congress slams Tharoor for showing ‘admiration’ for Modi; MP says statement given ‘partisan twist’
content: The row was sparked by Shashi Tharoor's reported remarks that in meetings with U.S. President Donald Trump, PM Modi conveyed that in wartime, civilian sailors on commercial ships should not be targets. Pawan Khera says Tharoor can now hear what Modi doesn’t even say.

title: India is now fourth largest economy, says govt.
content: The government says that with the GDP valued at $4.18 trillion, “India has surpassed Japan to become the world’s fourth largest economy”, it says that the country is “poised to displace Germany from the third rank” with a projected GDP of $7.3 trillion by 2030

title: CJP protest in Delhi: Dipke, supporters continue sit-in overnight at Jantar Mantar; urge people to join protest
content: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk, who joined the protest on June 20, announced that he would go on a hunger strike on June 27 if Union Education Miniser Dharmendra Pradhan did not step down

title: Modi’s 12 years in office just a start: Amit Shah
content: Union Home Minister lavishes praise on Prime Minister Narendra Modi, says BJP and NDA will rule the country for many years to come; highlights Centre’s role in temple developments, military modernisation and farmers’ welfare

title: Decoupling footpaths from accidents
content: Justice Narasimha’s judgment divorces the footpath from the theme of motor accidents. The sole objective of a footpath is not to avoid accidents. The pathways have an identity and a greater purpose of their own

title: Do the police need court permission for further investigation after filing the final report?
content: Although an application seeking further investigation had been filed before the concerned magistrate for a third time, no order granting permission was placed on record, the Court said

title: Strong maritime capabilities deciding factor for country’s economic, strategic influence: PM Modi
content: West Bengal Governor R.N. Ravi, Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari, and Indian Navy Chief Admiral Krishna Swaminathan were among the dignitaries present at the commissioning event

title: Beyond books: How a Kolkata school is teaching girls to build self-reliant futures
content: The residential school for girls uses practical learning and skill development to prepare students from rural communities for self-sustaining livelihoods

title: Engineer’s body recovered from Varthur STP; search continues for another missing worker
content: The search is still underway for Brijesh Kumar, a worker from Bihar, who also went missing after the incident on Thursday evening.

title: Permanent yoga centres to be set up in A.P.’s Swarna Grama and Ward Secretariats: CM Naidu
content: He was joined by Baba Ramdev at Yoga Day event in Vijayawada; the CM said Prime Minister Modi has brought global recognition to yoga

title: Sonam Wangchuk calls himself ‘honorary cockroach’, urges government to hear voices of youth
content: Voicing support for the online cockroach movement by the self-styled Cockroach Janata Party, Sonam Wangchuk said the campaign should be viewed as a form of democratic feedback and not as a threat

title: I don’t think the ruling party has the stomach for sarcasm, says Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke
content: Abhijeet Dipke, founder of the ‘Cockroach Janta Party’, alleges threats and censorship following the takedown of his youth-led satirical political outfit’s website

title: Amid global uncertainties, parliamentary panel to study evolving economic conditions in India
content: The examination is expected to cover topics, including economic growth, inflation, employment, investment trends, fiscal management, banking sector developments, trade and the impact of global developments on India

title: Centre offering Ladakh unique governance model, demand for Statehood to continue: Leh Apex Body leader
content: Cherring Dorjay Lakruk, co-convenor of the Leh Apex Body, says, “We are not abandoning our demand for Statehood. Even if at present, Statehood is not possible, we will continue with the demand.”

title: CID questions Trinamool veteran Sobhandeb in signature forgery case
content: He was the party’s original choice as the Leader of Opposition before a majority of MLAs sided with Ritabrata Banerjee

title: Thousands join International Yoga Day events worldwide
content: International Day of Yoga is celebrated globally on June 21 every year, since its adoption by the United Nations in 2014

title: Tales TDS tells: Farmers in Hosur allege chemical discharge from TEPL plant causing low yield
content: Farmers in Hosur have accused the Tata Electronics plant of discharging effluents into farmlands and contaminating the water table. This report takes a deep dive to study the situation on the ground

title: Conspiracy being hatched to kill me, my father: Tej Pratap
content: Tej Pratap Yadav has lodged a police complaint against former aide Akash Yadav and the latter’s family members; he requested Bihar Chief Minister Samrat Choudhary to provide him with adequate security

title: Ladakh civil society groups call for shutdown over ‘Centre’s backtracking on May 22 agreement’
content: All shops and business centres will remain closed on June 23 while only transport will be allowed because of the ongoing tourist season, says Ladakh Apex Body chairman in a joint press conference with Kargil Democratic Alliance

title: IUML ends alliance with the DMK after six decades
content: Since the IUML has become part of the Ministry, it will cooperate with the TVK in Assembly byelections, Lok Sabha elections, and local body polls, he says

title: India rejects Pakistan President Zardari’s remarks on Kashmir
content: Jaiswal said Pakistan's "long history of systematically targeting and victimising minorities across various faiths is notorious."

title: Telegram | Mass messaging and more
content: The ‘WhatsApp alternative’ with a strong commitment to user privacy, which has over 15 crore users in India, is facing a week-long ban in the country

title: CJP presses for Pradhan’s resignation at second Jantar Mantar protest
content: Despite police calls to evacuate after the 5 p.m. deadline, CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke and many protestors stayed back and said he would continue to stay until Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan resigned.

title: NEET-UG 2026: Over 6,000 observers to ensure ‘fair, secure’ retest on June 21, NTA says
content: 22 lakh aspirants likely to attend the exam after previous test was cancelled over paper leak allegations; CCTV, jammers installed at all exam centres; additional staff and invigilators deployed

title: 100 years of Mehboob: A legacy that lives on
content: World Music Day: Generations of music lovers reminisce the stories and songs of maverick musician H Mehboob

title: Crooners beat the commuting blues on a long suburban line from Chennai to Tiruvallur
content: Every day, at a set hour in the morning and evening, a compartment in the local electric train on the Chennai Central-Tiruvallur suburban line morphs into a concert hall. Listening in to the voices from this decades-old amateur train singing group, clearly a Methuselah among such groups across the Chennai Metropolitan Area

title: International Yoga Day 2026: PM Modi to lead nation from Kolkata
content: Yoga Day celebrations are being organised across nearly 2,500 locations worldwide, with participation from more than 210 Indian missions and posts

title: Indian man jailed for over 5 years in U.K.-France people smuggling case
content: Jaskirat Singh was prosecuted by the U.K.'s Crown Prosecution Service (CPS) over his role in facilitating the illegal movement of non-British nationals between December 2024 and March 2026

title: Number of patients in Bhiwandi food poisoning rises to 122
content: According to the police, several customers who had eaten food items at a local establishment on June 17 experienced stomachache and diarrhoea the following day and had to be rushed to hospitals

title: Insuring the informal worker against the heat
content: ‘Parametric’ schemes pay out when temperatures cross a threshold, but workers value the cash without taking the day off

title: Regular yoga practice makes healthy ageing possible: AIIMS
content: With the world’s elderly population rapidly expanding, making healthy ageing a public health priority is more critical than ever, says the AIIMS Professor

title: Employee letters disbursed under Pradhan Mantri Viksit Bharat Rojgar Yojana in Chennai
content: At IMAGE, a total of 750 participants including 450 beneficiaries, 122 employers and industry association representatives participated

title: NHAI to request Vellore Corporation to build wide culvert on Chenganatham Hill Road to prevent water stagnation
content: Areas like Rangapuram and Mullakollai, that come under ward 24 of Zone-II of Vellore Corporation, are located along the stretch

title: GRP seize 12 kg of ganja at Jolarpet railway station
content: Initial inquiry revealed that a gang of three persons abandoned the consignment and fled the spot after they noticed a police team get onto the train

title: T.N. government opts for direct recruitment to address shortage of fireman drivers
content: Earlier, the post, which is crucial for operation of fire fighting equipment, was filled up only promotion of personnel within the service

title: Pawan Kalyan bats for online citizens’ forum to question abuse of law and order, if any
content: The subject is not under my jurisdiction, but I will speak on it with the civil society, he says, and observes that criminals should not be elected to power

title: Stress takes a toll on students ahead of NEET re-exam
content: While many students are hoping that the re-exam will be held with no glitches, most point out that they have lost hope in a fair examination

title: Similipal’s black tigers are getting help
content: Zeenat, a tigress translocated from Maharashtra’s Tadoba-Andhari Tiger Reserve to Odisha’s Similipal Tiger Reserve two years ago, has given birth to four cubs. Satyasundar Barik speaks to the people behind the high-stakes conservation effort to rescue Similipal’s tigers from a genetic mutation that has fuelled the rise of pseudo-melanism, or dense, thick, merging stripes

title: Tamil Nadu to roll out Intellectual Property Rights policy with focus on GI products
content: During the financial year 2025-26, the State had filed a total of 43 Geographical Indication registration applications; of these, 11 had been filed by the Patent Information Centre under the TNSCST; expert stresses the need to integrate GI products with the tourism sector to enhance their visibility

title: Lt. Gen. Dhiraj Seth | Officer at the helm
content: The new Army Chief assumes command at a juncture marked by rapid technological advances, evolving security challenges, and a growing emphasis on joint operations

title: Group of Seven | A platform for the globalised elite
content: The grouping of industrialised nations, founded in the 1970s, has weathered major geopolitical storms across the 20th and 21st centuries to remain a key forum for discussions and decision-making on global conflicts and economic challenges

title: Thenmala Punarjani shelter home case: Kerala HC expresses concern over unregistered elderly care institutions
content: HC Bench raises issue while considering a petition registered after reports of alleged ill treatment of residents at a shelter home, Punarjani in Thenmala, surfaced

title: Satya Nadella's new inner circle: Meet Microsoft's key leaders
content: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella has restructured the senior leadership team that ran Microsoft for decades, replacing it with leaner, startup-style teams built for the AI era. Business Insider reports the SLT is gone, swapped for a five-person corporate group, a 35-strong engineering team and a dedicated Copilot unit. Nadella now reviews AI metrics weekly. Longtime executives including Rajesh Jha, Yusuf Mehdi and Charlie Bell are out as Microsoft's AI reboot reshapes its power structure.

title: Happy Father’s Day 2026: Top 50 wishes, messages and quotes to make dad feel special
content: Fathers often express love through actions, not words, and Father's Day on June 21, 2026, is a time to appreciate their guidance and support. Simple gestures like cooking a favorite meal or sharing memories can mean more than gifts. This day is about acknowledging their sacrifices and letting them know they are deeply appreciated.

title: Sooryavanshi scores fastest List A fifty, but 324.14 strike rate isn't his best
content: Young prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, just 15, delivered a sensational 94 off 29 balls in the Tri-Nation Series final against Sri Lanka A. This explosive innings, featuring a world-record 11-ball fifty, highlights his immense talent.  However, it's not his fastest knock, with a 144 off 42 balls against UAE in 2025 holding that record, showcasing his consistent brilliance.

title: UK detains Indian captain of sanctioned ship, family in Uttarakhand pleads for release
content: An Indian captain, Ajay Pant, has been arrested in the UK and charged with breaching sanctions after his Russian oil tanker was intercepted in the English Channel. The vessel, en route to India, was found to be stateless. Pant's family in Uttarakhand has appealed to the Indian government for his safe return, while authorities are coordinating efforts for his release.

title: Palantir CEO roasts Mamdani voters over their 'less valuable' Ivy League degree
content: Palantir CEO Alex Karp has publicly criticized New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani, suggesting that Ivy League graduates who support him feel their education is devalued compared to practical professions. Karp also claimed that many Democrats privately express doubts about Mamdani's agenda, urging them to speak out.  The tech executive also touched upon the critical AI race and his personal sleep habits.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi scripts history, hits fastest fifty in List A cricket
content: Indian prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi achieved a historic feat in the Tri-Nation Series final, smashing the fastest fifty in List A cricket history. The 15-year-old opener blazed to his half-century in a mere 11 deliveries against Sri Lanka A. This electrifying performance, marked by powerful hitting, shattered the previous world record and showcased the young talent's remarkable prowess on the international stage.

title: Anthropic's 3k employees have one question after US 'banned' its most powerful AI models
content: Anthropic's 3,000 employees are still confused nearly a week after the White House gave the company under 90 minutes to pull its most powerful AI models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, over vague national security concerns. As The New York Times reported from internal chats, staff suspect they're being targeted rather than reviewed. Triggered by an Amazon paper, the shutdown has left engineers asking whether the Trump administration simply doesn't want Anthropic to exist.

title: 'We don't get to choose..': What Google CEO Sundar Pichai advices to students
content: Google CEO Sundar Pichai, addressing Stanford's Class of 2026, urged graduates to embrace optimism and tackle challenging endeavors. He shared personal anecdotes, emphasizing that meaningful work stems from genuine passion rather than external expectations. Pichai's core message highlighted the importance of perseverance and continuous forward movement, regardless of life's inevitable uncertainties.

title: Is your landlord not returning security deposit? Know your rights and what counts as ‘damage’
content: Resolving security deposit disputes in India is all about knowing your rights as a tenant and the duties of landlords. The law makes a clear distinction between normal wear and tear—covered by landlords—and tenant-caused damages. To support claims for deductions, landlords must provide strong evidence, including photos and receipts, to facilitate a fair and efficient resolution between parties involved.

title: 'Development, security, prosperity linked to sea': PM Modi commissions 3 naval ships
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi commissioned three indigenously built naval ships – INS Dunagiri, INS Sanshodhak, and INS Agray – in Kolkata, marking a significant stride in India's maritime self-reliance. These vessels, boasting over 75% indigenous content, will bolster the nation's naval prowess in combat, surveying, and anti-submarine warfare, reinforcing India's growing influence on the global maritime stage.

title: NEET paper leak claim: Caller shows ‘question paper’ on video call, demands Rs 30k
content: Ajmer police are investigating an alleged attempt to sell the NEET question paper. A student reported receiving a video call from an unidentified person displaying the paper and demanding Rs 30,000. A local ABVP member also received a similar offer. Authorities are working to identify the caller and confirm the paper's authenticity, suspecting a fraudulent scheme ahead of the NEET re-exam.

title: CJP overnight protest: Dipke claims authorities cut off water, electricity at Jantar Mantar
content: Protesters at Jantar Mantar, demanding Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over examination controversies, face water supply issues in restrooms. The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) highlighted the lack of basic facilities for demonstrators, contrasting it with the minister's situation. Meanwhile, the NEET-UG 2026 re-examination is underway nationwide, with CJP inviting aspirants to join their protest post-exam.

title: Who will take wickets in middle overs? Gill pins hope on tall fast bowlers
content: India's search for consistent middle-over wickets has prompted a shift in strategy, with Shubman Gill backing tall fast bowlers like Gurnoor Brar, Prasidh Krishna and Harshit Rana to shoulder the responsibility. The captain explained why India is prioritising pace, bounce and flexibility over fixed bowling roles ahead of the 2027 World Cup.

title: 1.4L CCTVs, 51k jammers, 22.8L candidates: NEET re-test under massive security cover
content: Over 22 lakh students are appearing for the NEET (UG) 2026 re-exam today across India and abroad. The National Testing Agency has deployed extensive security measures, including CCTVs and jammers, at 5,440 centres. Authorities are investigating an alleged attempt to sell the question paper in Ajmer. Meanwhile, a student's exam centre location dispute in Abu Dhabi is also being addressed.

title: How Suvendu Sarkar, raised without parental love, became mother and father to his kids
content: Suvendu Sarkar, who lost his mother at two and was abandoned by his father, found solace in his own family until tragedy struck again in 2021 when he lost his wife to COVID-19. Left to raise his infant son and daughter alone, Suvendu defied doubts and societal expectations, embracing the roles of both mother and father.

title: Room makes record for most saves as Curacao earn first-ever FIFA WC point
content: The result also confirmed Germany's place in the knockout stage after their 2-1 win over Ivory Coast earlier in the day.

title: How to increase the resale value of your house: Tips for homeowners
content: Homeowners can significantly boost their property's selling price with smart, cost-effective upgrades. Focus on enhancing curb appeal with a clean exterior and well-maintained garden. Inside, a fresh coat of neutral paint and deep cleaning create a brighter, more spacious feel. Prioritizing kitchen and bathroom updates, along with essential repairs, builds buyer confidence. Energy-efficient features also add appeal, but avoid over-improving beyond neighbourhood expectations to ensure a better return.

title: 'We are all grateful to PM Modi': Top ministers, CMs perform yoga across India
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi led International Yoga Day celebrations in Kolkata, emphasizing "Yoga for Healthy Ageing" and its role in maintaining physical and mental well-being. Across India, Union ministers and chief ministers participated in mass yoga sessions, highlighting yoga's global reach and its contribution to a healthier, more vibrant nation. The day marked a collective global celebration of yoga's benefits.

title: Yashasvi Jaiswal leaves Virat Kohli, Shubman Gill behind in major ODI record
content: Yashasvi Jaiswal etched his name in history, becoming the fastest Indian to score two ODI centuries in just six innings with a brilliant unbeaten 110 against Afghanistan. His stellar knock, coupled with Rohit Sharma's 79, guided India to a commanding nine-wicket victory and a 3-0 series whitewash.  Prasidh Krishna's five-wicket haul earlier restricted Afghanistan to 218, setting the stage for Jaiswal's record-breaking performance.

title: 'More flexible at 40, energetic at 50': PM Modi on healthy ageing, yoga's benefits - top quotes
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi urged citizens to integrate yoga into their daily lives, not just observe it on a single day, during International Yoga Day celebrations in Kolkata. He emphasized yoga's role as a global movement fostering physical health, mental well-being, and emotional resilience for all ages, particularly highlighting 'Yoga for Healthy Ageing'.

title: 22.8 lakh to take NEET-UG re-test today across India
content: Over 22 lakh students are set to appear for the NEET (UG) 2026 re-exam nationwide. Uttar Pradesh and Maharashtra lead with the highest number of candidates, while Rajasthan emerges as a significant coaching hub. A striking majority, nearly 80%, will take the test in English, with Hindi a distant second. Southern states maintain a strong presence, and regional languages see limited participation.

title: Protests mount in Bangladesh over alleged desecration of Hindu deity
content: Hindu communities across Bangladesh are protesting alleged government inaction following an incident where a picture of Lord Ram was reportedly desecrated. Leaders expressed deep concern over repeated attacks on minorities, emphasizing their rights as citizens and vowing to resist "evil forces" threatening religious harmony. Demonstrations, including human chains and rallies, demand swift legal action against perpetrators.

title: NEET UG 2026 re-test: Multi-layered checks in place; control room to monitor process in Gujarat
content: Medical aspirants in Gujarat are gearing up for the NEET re-exam this Sunday following a paper leak. Unprecedented security measures are in place, including GPS-tracked question paper transport, CCTV surveillance at all 211 centres, and rigorous frisking for over 79,000 students. Authorities have established a central control room and deployed senior police officials to ensure a smooth and secure examination process, aiming to prevent any malpractice.

title: Gill wins first ODI series as captain; equals Rohit Sharma's feat
content: Shubman Gill captained India to a commanding 3-0 ODI series victory against Afghanistan, marking his first series win as skipper. The young batsman also shone with the bat, finishing as the highest run-scorer with 238 runs and earning Player of the Series.  Gill praised his team's execution and highlighted the upcoming England series as crucial preparation for the 2027 World Cup.

title: Shashi Tharoor's praise for PM Modi sparks political slugfest
content: Congress MP Shashi Tharoor's assertion that Prime Minister Modi raised concerns about Indian civilian sailors with US President Trump has sparked internal party friction.  This statement contradicts Rahul Gandhi's claim that the issue was ignored.  The BJP has seized on Tharoor's remarks, highlighting the divergence within Congress and his praise for the Prime Minister's leadership.

title: Gill breaks silence on Rohit's ODI future: 'It is a good kind of headache'
content: Shubman Gill admitted India have "a good kind of headache" over the opening combination after Yashasvi Jaiswal's second ODI hundred in three matches intensified the selection debate around Rohit Sharma ahead of the England tour. While backing Jaiswal's talent, Gill said the final XI would depend on the squad and players' fitness.

title: UK PM Keir Starmer expected to announce resignation on Monday: Report
content: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is reportedly poised to announce his resignation on Monday, following mounting pressure after Andy Burnham's significant by-election win.  Discussions with key figures suggest Starmer is considering a departure timetable.  While he previously vowed to fight any challenge, allies of Burnham and Wes Streeting are urging a smooth transition.  Starmer has been assessing his support within his cabinet.

title: FSSAI targets misleading ads as brands race to sell ‘healthy’ food
content: India's food regulator, FSSAI, is cracking down on misleading health claims on food and supplement labels. Products marketed as "natural," "healthy," or "no added sugar" are under scrutiny for potentially deceiving consumers. This intensified enforcement follows rising complaints and social media attention, aiming to ensure transparency and protect public health by urging consumers to look beyond catchy marketing phrases.

title: At Lahore's 'Eton', Pakistani alum pays tribute to Indian dost
content: A classroom at Lahore's Aitchison College, once attended by leaders from undivided Punjab, now bridges India and Pakistan.  A tribute to former Punjab CM Harcharan Singh Brar, funded by his 100-year-old schoolmate Syed Babar Ali, highlights enduring friendships forged before Partition. This initiative is part of a larger effort to remember the institution's pre-1947 past, honouring students and teachers scattered by history.

title: CBSE to consult key stakeholders on digital marking system
content: CBSE's on-screen marking system for Class XII exams faces an uncertain future. The board will consult students, teachers, and parents before deciding on its continuation for 2027 and potential expansion to Class X.  This review follows complaints about the system's debut, including scanning issues and unevaluated answers, and aims to address gaps exposed during recent exams and rebuild trust.

title: Indian, Pak Army officers shake hands during a repatriation process along LoC
content: In a gesture of goodwill, the Indian Army repatriated a Pakistani national, Asad Khan, apprehended along the Line of Control in Kupwara last week. Khan, who reportedly crossed the LoC inadvertently while seeking employment, was treated with dignity and compassion. The handover at the Aman Setu peace bridge signifies a potential easing of tensions, underscoring the Indian Army's commitment to humanitarian values and professionalism.

title: Row erupts over Vijay songs, TVK campaign music at Coimbatore medical college convocations
content: Convocation ceremonies at ESI and Coimbatore Medical Colleges became a point of contention as film songs featuring actor Vijay and ruling TVK election campaign tunes blared.  "Raavana Mavanda" and "Oru Pere Varalaru" from Vijay's upcoming film, alongside TVK campaign anthems, were played, drawing criticism for a perceived lack of discipline at government events. Netizens expressed dismay, calling the administration a "joke."

title: India Gets 3 New Indigenous Warships, Here's Why They Are Special
content: The three naval ships are stealth frigate Dunagiri, survey vessel (large) Sanshodhak, and anti-submarine warfare shallow water craft Agray

title: Spain PM's Wife To Face Corruption Trial, Told To Surrender Passport
content: Spanish PM Pedro Sanchez's wife Begona Gomez is accused of using her position to influence government contracts given to a group of technology companies.

title: "Misanthropy": Millions In Brazil Get Fake Government Mobile Alert, Hack Suspected
content: Officials said the false messages were "remotely ordered by someone outside the national system of protection and civil defense," and delivered to "various regions of the country."

title: Light Rain Brings Relief In Mumbai After Monsoon Delay
content: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has indicated favourable conditions for further advancement of the southwest monsoon over parts of Maharashtra.

title: In A First For Monarchs, King Charles To Reveal Personal Tax Bill
content: British monarchs are legally exempt from paying certain taxes, though they have paid some duties on a voluntary basis for decades.

title: "What Have You Done?" Road Rage Shocker In Gurugram, 2 Students Arrested
content: The two college students allegedly stopped the victim's MG Winsor car, smashed the windowpanes, and assaulted the driver with sticks.

title: PM Modi Leads International Yoga Day Event From Kolkata's Iconic Red Road
content: The 12th edition of the International Day of Yoga is being observed across the country and around the world on Sunday under the theme 'Yoga for Healthy Ageing'

title: 'Be More Energetic At 50 Than You Were At 30': PM's Top Quotes At Yoga Event
content: 12th International Day of Yoga: PM Modi said June 21, the longest day on the Earth, is now recognised worldwide as a day of yoga.

title: 22 Lakh Students, 5,440 Centres, 1 Lakh Cameras: High-Stakes NEET Re-Exam Today
content: The re-test will be held from 2 pm to 5.15 pm. Candidates with disabilities, who are eligible for compensatory time, will be allowed time till 6.20 pm.

title: "Bigotry, Hatred": India Slams Pak Over President Zardari's "Absurd" Remark
content: New Delhi has once again reminded Islamabad that it has no locus standi to speak on its neighbour's internal matters.

title: US-Iran War News Live Updates: JD Vance Lands In Switzerland For Iran Talks
content: US-Iran Peace Talks Live Updates: JD Vance had on Thursday cancelled his trip to Switzerland amid rising tension in Lebanon between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah.

title: US-Iran Resume Dialogue Today As Trump Warns Of Tolls On Hormuz If Talks Fail
content: Donald Trump and his Iranian counterpart Masoud Pezeshkian on Wednesday signed a 14-point deal to end the war in the Middle East.

title: What To Gift Dad Who Wants Nothing? ChatGPT Builds A Budget-Wise List
content: Looking for Father's Day gift ideas? ChatGPT suggests thoughtful, budget-friendly gifts for dads who say they want nothing.

title: Pics: Texas To Times Square, Yoga Day Sees Huge Participation Across US
content: Yoga is practiced by tens of millions of Americans and has become one of the most visible examples of India's cultural influence in the country.

title: "Yoga Now World's Largest Community Celebration": PM Modi On Yoga Day
content: Speaking at the 12th International Day of Yoga celebrations in Kolkata, PM Modi said June 21, the longest day on the Earth, is now recognised worldwide as a day of yoga.

title: Iran Closes Strait Of Hormuz Again Over Israel's Attacks On Lebanon
content: Iran said that the closure was the "first step" in response to what it described as breaches of commitments.

title: Israeli Army Confirms Journalist Killed In Gaza, Calls Him 'Hamas' Terrorist
content: The Israeli military said Saturday it had carried out a strike that killed Al Jazeera journalist Ahmed Wishah in Gaza

title: 'He Hears What PM Doesn't Say': Congress, Shashi Tharoor In Fresh Bout
content: Pawan Khera took up the mantle to chastise his senior colleague. His message, though sarcastic, conveyed the party's stern stance on Tharoor, drawing a sharp response from the MP.

title: Woman Stabbed To Death, Husband Injured In Fight Over Parking In Delhi
content: During preliminary inquiry, the police found that the couple had got into a fight with their neighbours over the parking of a motorcycle on the road.

content: the Indian Navy is scheduled to commission three indigenously-built frontline platforms . the platforms will bolster India's blue-water capability, shallow-water anti-submarine operations, and oceanographic surveying . GRSE was granted the 'Navratna' status on Friday .

title: Modi a tough cookie, 1 of my 2 favourite world leaders: Trump
